title: podman farm list should not require an "engine" (connection)
body:
### Feature request description

Currently when you try to distribute builds with `podman-remote`, it _still_ requires a local socket with v4.9.0

You can't even do farm list:

`Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM`

Only edit containers.conf.

### Suggest potential solution

I think that at least `podman-remote farm list` shouldn't require a connection, probably only "build" needs one ?

### Have you considered any alternatives?

`cat ~/.config/containers/containers.conf`

### Additional context

Looking at "system connection list", it seems that you can no-op the engine creation.

```diff
diff --git a/cmd/podman/farm/list.go b/cmd/podman/farm/list.go
index eb13b6d48..26742f276 100644
--- a/cmd/podman/farm/list.go
+++ b/cmd/podman/farm/list.go
@@ -25,7 +25,9 @@ and the output format can be changed to JSON or a user specified Go template.`
                Args:               validate.NoArgs,
                Short:              "List all existing farms",
                Long:               farmLsDescription,
+               PersistentPreRunE:  validate.NoOp,
                RunE:               list,
+               PersistentPostRunE: validate.NoOp,
                ValidArgsFunction:  completion.AutocompleteNone,
        }
 
```

cc @umohnani8

comments.body: I would assume this applies to farm add,rm,update, etc... as well. I think even build does not need it depending on what connections are selected.
Some of the other commands do a "test call", just to see that the connection name that you provide is still working (or something like that?)
But maybe it should be handled the same way (as "system connection", disabled in the top "farm" command and then enabled again where it is needed?
